title: Treatment of developmental syndromes
abstract: A method of treating a developmental syndrome in a patient in need thereof includes applying ultrasound to a target location in the patient's brain to enhance permeability of the patient's blood brain barrier at the target location and administering to the patient a vector encoding BDNF for delivery of BDNF to the target location, wherein the method provides improvement in at least one symptom of the developmental syndrome. Also provided is a method of treating a developmental syndrome in a patient in need thereof that includes administering to the patient an effective amount of a vector including (i) a constitutive promoter operatively linked to nucleic acid encoding BDNF, and (ii) a regulatory sequence including an AGRP promoter operatively linked to an interference RNA sequence, wherein the regulatory sequence down regulates expression of BDNF in response to BDNF induced physiological changes, and the method provides improvement in at least one symptom of the developmental syndrome.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of treating a developmental syndrome in a patient in need thereof comprising: applying ultrasound to a target location in the patient's brain to enhance permeability of the patient's blood brain barrier at the target location; administering to the patient a vector encoding BDNF for delivery of BDNF to the target location, wherein the method provides improvement in at least one symptom of the developmental syndrome. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the ultrasound is administered through the skull. 3. The method according to claim 1, further comprising exposing dura matter of the brain and delivering the ultrasound at or below the exposed dura matter. 4. The method according to claim 1, wherein the ultrasound is administered to the target location in the patient's brain at a frequency ranging from 20 kHz to 10 MHz, sonication duration ranging from 100 nanoseconds to 30 minutes, with continuous wave or burst mode operation, where the burst mode repetition varies from 0.01 Hz to 1 MHz. 5. The method according to claim 1, wherein the ultrasound is administered to the target location in the patient's brain at a frequency ranging from 250 kHz to 10 MHz, and with sonication duration ranging from 0.10 microseconds to 30 minutes. 6. The method according to claim 1, wherein the ultrasound is administered to the target location in the patient's brain at a frequency ranging from 20 kHz to 5 MHz, and with sonication duration ranging from 100 nanoseconds to 1 minute. 7. The method according to claim 1, wherein the ultrasound is administered to the target location in the patient's brain prior to administering the vector. 8. The method according to claim 1, further comprising introducing a contrast agent into the patient, allowing sufficient time for the contrast agent to permeate the blood brain barrier and determining whether the contrast agent is present in the target location. 9. The method according to claim 1, wherein the vector is an adeno-associated virus. 10. The method according to claim 9, wherein the adeno-associated virus is AAV 1 , AA V2, AA V4, AAV5, A .V7, AAV 8 or AAV9. 11. The method according to claim 9, wherein the adeno-associated virus is AAV 1 or AAV2. 12. The method according to claim 1, wherein the vector is a lentivirus. 13. The method according to claim 1, wherein the vector is administered via a route selected from the group consisting of oral, buccal, sublingual, rectal, topical, intranasal, vaginal and parenteral. 14. The method according to claim 1, wherein the vector is administered prior to applying the ultrasound. 15. The method according to claim 1, wherein the developmental syndrome is selected from the group consisting of Prader-Willi syndrome, 16pl 1.2 deletion syndrome, 16pl 1.2 recurrent microdeletion, Albright hereditary osteodystrophy, Alstrom Syndrome, Bardet-Biedl syndrome, Borjeson-Forssman-Lehmann syndrome, Cohen syndrome, fragile X syndrome, fragile X syndrome (Prader-Willi Subtype), FMRl -Related Disorders, Down syndrome, Klinefelter syndrome, Turner syndrome, Smith-Magenis syndrome, Angelman syndrome, 21-Hydroxylase-Deficient Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia, 2q37 Microdeletion syndrome, 3q29 Microdeletion syndrome, Achondroplasia, ADNP Syndrome, melanocortin 4 receptor (MC4R) disorder, melanocortin 4 receptor (MC4R) deficiency, Proopiomelanocortin Deficiency, 15q24 Microdeletion Syndrome, 15q Duplication Syndrome and Related Disorders, lp36 Deletion Syndrome, and KANSL1 -Related Intellectual Disability Syndrome. 16. The method according to claim 1, wherein the symptom is selected from the group consisting of hypotonia, difficulty in sucking, difficulty in feeding, poor muscle tone, growth hormone deficiency, low levels of sex hormones, a constant feeling of hunger, excessive appetite (hyperphagia), weight gain, obesity, short stature, poor motor skills, underdeveloped sex organs, intellectual disability, learning disability, delayed speech development, delayed language development, hypothalamic dysphasia, infertility, cognitive rigidity, cognitive impairment, emotional lability, obsessive-compulsive behavior, autistic symptomology, psychotic episodes, bipolar disorder with psychosis, excessive daytime sleepiness, scoliosis, osteopenia/osteoporosis, decreased gastrointestinal motility, sleep disturbances, and reduced pain sensitivity. 17. The method according to claim 1, wherein the developmental syndrome is melanocortin 4 receptor (MC4R) disorder. 18. The method according to claim 1, wherein the target location is a hypothalamus. 19. The method according to claim 1, wherein the vector includes (i) a constitutive promoter operatively linked to nucleic acid encoding the BDNF, and (ii) a regulatory sequence including an AGRP promoter operatively linked to an interference RNA sequence, wherein the regulatory sequence down regulates expression of the BDNF in response to BDNF induced physiological changes. 20. The method according to claim 19, wherein the interference RNA sequence is selected from the group consisting of miRNA, siRNA and shRNA. 21. The method according to claim 19, wherein the interference RNA sequence is miR-Bdnf (SEQ ID NO:9). 22. The method according to claim 19, wherein the interference RNA sequence is operatively linked to WPRE1 (SEQ ID NO. 3) or WPRE155 (SEQ ID NO. 4) 23. The method according to claim 19, wherein the AGRP promoter is AGRP4M or AGRP814. 24. The method according to claim 19, further comprising WPRE which is operatively linked to the nucleic acid encoding BDNF. 25. The method according to claim 1, wherein the BDNF is human BDNF. 26. The method according to claim 19, wherein the constitutive promoter is a chicken Î²-actin promoter. 27. The method according to claim 19, wherein the constitutive promoter is CAG including a CMV early enhancer element, a chicken Î²-actin promoter and a splice acceptor of a rabbit beta-globin gene. 28. The method according to claim 19, wherein the vector includes a CMV enhancer and a chicken Î²-actin promoter, WPRE and bovine growth hormone poly-A flanked by AAV inverted terminal repeats. 29. The method according to claim 19, wherein the BDNF is flanked by two loxP sites (flox- BDNF), and the BDNF can be knocked out by Cre recombinase. 30. A method of treating a developmental syndrome in a patient in need thereof comprising: administering to the patient an effective amount of a vector including (i) a constitutive promoter operatively linked to nucleic acid encoding BDNF, and (ii) a regulatory sequence including 

 an AGRP promoter operatively linked to an interference RNA sequence, wherein the regulatory sequence down regulates expression of BDNF in response to BDNF induced physiological changes, and the method provides improvement in at least one symptom of the developmental syndrome. 31. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the developmental syndrome is selected from the group consisting of Prader-Willi syndrome, 16pl 1.2 deletion syndrome, 16pl 1.2 recurrent microdeletion, Albright hereditary osteodystrophy, Alstrom Syndrome, Bardet-Biedl syndrome, Borjeson-Forssman-Lehmann syndrome, Cohen syndrome, fragile X syndrome, fragile X syndrome (Prader-Willi Subtype), FMR1 -Related Disorders, Down syndrome, Klinefelter syndrome, Turner syndrome, Smith-Magenis syndrome, Angelman syndrome, 21- Hydroxylase-Deficient Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia, 2q37 Microdeletion syndrome, 3q29 Microdeletion syndrome, Achondroplasia, ADNP Syndrome, melanocortin 4 receptor (MC4R) disorder, melanocortin 4 receptor (MC4R) deficiency, Proopiomelanocortin Deficiency, 15q24 Microdeletion Syndrome, 15q Duplication Syndrome and Related Disorders, lp36 Deletion Syndrome, and KANSL1 -Related Intellectual Disability Syndrome. 32. The method according to claim 30, wherein the developmental syndrome is melanocortin 4 receptor (MC4R) disorder. 33. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the symptom is selected from the group consisting of hypotonia, difficulty in sucking, difficulty in feeding, poor muscle tone, growth hormone deficiency, low levels of sex hormones, a constant feeling of hunger, excessive appetite (hyperphagia), weight gain, obesity, short stature, poor motor skills, underdeveloped sex organs, intellectual disability, learning disability, delayed speech development, delayed language development, hypothalamic dysphasia, infertility, cognitive rigidity, cognitive impairment, emotional lability, obsessive-compulsive behavior, autistic symptomology, psychotic episodes, bipolar disorder with psychosis, excessive daytime sleepiness, scoliosis, osteopenia/osteoporosis, decreased gastrointestinal motility, sleep disturbances, and reduced pain sensitivity 34. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the interference RNA sequence is selected from the group consisting of miRNA, siRNA and shRNA. 35. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the interference RNA sequence is miR-Bdnf (SEQ ID NO: 9). 36. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the interference RNA sequence is operatively linked to WPRE1A (SEQ ID NO. 3) or WPRE155 (SEQ ID NO. 4) 37. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the AGRP promoter is AGRP484 or AGRP814. 38. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, further comprising WPRE which is operatively linked to the nucleic acid encoding BDNF. 39. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the BDNF is human BDNF. 40. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the constitutive promoter is a chicken Î²-actin promoter. 41. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the constitutive promoter is CAG including a CMV early enhancer element, a chicken Î²-actin promoter and a splice acceptor of a rabbit beta-globin gene. 42. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the vector includes a CMV enhancer and a chicken Î²-actin promoter, WPRE and bovine growth hormone poly-A flanked by AAV inverted terminal repeats. 43. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the BDNF is flanked by two loxP sites (flox-BDNF), and the BDNF can be knocked out by Cre recombinase. 44. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the vector is administered via a route selected from the group consisting of oral, buccal, sublingual, rectal, topical, intranasal, vaginal and parenteral. 45. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, further comprising administering ultrasound to a target location in the patient's brain to enhance permeability of the patient's blood brain barrier at the target location. 46. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 45, wherein the ultrasound is administered through the skull. 47. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 45, wherein the ultrasound is administered to a hypothalamus. 48. The method of treating a developmental syndrome according to claim 30, wherein the vector is administered to a hypothalamus.
